prompts.title: Shaft Design Calculation Walkthrough
prompts.prompt: Act as a Design of Machine Elements professor with 15 years of experience teaching at NIT Trichy, closely aligned with the GATE ME syllabus and Shigley's textbook. I need you to solve a complete shaft design problem step by step so I can understand both the theory and the numerical procedure. Here is the context: I am a 5th semester mechanical engineering student at VIT Vellore working on my DoM assignment. The shaft transmits 25 kW at 720 rpm, is simply supported over 900 mm, carries a spur gear of 250 mm PCD at mid-span with a 20 degree pressure angle, and is made of C40 steel with Syt = 328 MPa. The shaft has a keyway and operates under gradually applied load. Please provide: (1) Free body diagram description with all forces resolved in vertical and horizontal planes, (2) Bending moment and torque calculations with diagrams described in text, (3) Equivalent twisting moment using ASME code with Kb and Kt factors, (4) Shaft diameter calculation with factor of safety 2, rounded to nearest standard size per IS 1239, (5) Check for rigidity using angle of twist less than 1 degree per metre. Format the output as a solved numerical with clearly labelled steps, units in SI throughout, and a final answer box. Consider these constraints: follow PSG Design Data Book conventions used in Indian engineering colleges, cite relevant IS codes, and flag where GATE questions commonly test this concept.

prompts.title: Thermodynamics Rankine Cycle Solver
prompts.prompt: Act as a thermodynamics faculty member with 12 years of experience at IIT Madras who has set GATE ME questions on power cycles. I need you to solve a complete Rankine cycle problem and explain each state point using steam tables. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student preparing for end-semester exams at Anna University. A steam power plant similar to NTPC Ramagundam operates on an ideal reheat Rankine cycle. Steam enters the HP turbine at 15 MPa and 600 C, expands to 2 MPa, is reheated to 600 C, then expands in the LP turbine to condenser pressure of 10 kPa. The mass flow rate is 80 kg/s. Please provide: (1) A clear state-point table with h, s, T, P, and quality x at each of the six state points using NIST or standard Indian steam tables, (2) Work output from HP and LP turbines separately, (3) Pump work and heat added in boiler and reheater, (4) Net power output in MW and thermal efficiency, (5) Comparison with the simple Rankine cycle efficiency at the same conditions, (6) Comment on how reheating affects turbine exit quality and blade erosion. Format the output as a numbered solution with a T-s diagram description, state table, and final results box. Consider these constraints: use SI units throughout, reference the steam tables as given in the R.K. Rajput or P.K. Nag textbook commonly used in Indian universities, and highlight concepts frequently tested in GATE ME.

prompts.title: SolidWorks Part Modeling Step Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a SolidWorks certified CSWA instructor with 10 years of experience training mechanical students at BITS Pilani. I need you to write a complete, click-by-click modeling guide for a specific part so I can follow along on my laptop. Here is the context: I am a 3rd year mechanical engineering student who just installed SolidWorks 2024 Student Edition. I need to model a flange coupling half as per the drawing in the Shigley textbook: bore 40 mm, hub OD 80 mm, hub length 60 mm, flange OD 160 mm, flange thickness 20 mm, 4 bolt holes of 14 mm on a 120 mm PCD, and a 12x8 mm keyway in the bore. Please provide: (1) Recommended sketch plane and origin setup, (2) Sequential feature tree with every sketch, revolve, extrude, hole wizard, and circular pattern explained in order, (3) Exact dimensions and constraints to apply at each step, (4) Where to use design intent like symmetric and equal relations so the model is parametric, (5) Material assignment as IS 2062 E250 structural steel with density for mass properties, (6) How to generate a 2D drawing with three views, section, and BOM-ready title block in Indian third angle projection. Format the output as a numbered tutorial with headings for each feature and a final checklist. Consider these constraints: follow SP 46 drawing standards used in Indian engineering curriculum and assume the student has only basic sketching experience.

prompts.title: Heat Transfer Fin Efficiency Problem
prompts.prompt: Act as a heat transfer professor with 14 years of experience at IIT Bombay who has authored problems for GATE ME. I need you to solve an extended surface heat transfer problem in full detail. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student at COEP Pune studying finned heat exchangers used in Bajaj air-cooled motorcycle engines. A rectangular aluminium fin (k = 205 W/mK) is 100 mm long, 50 mm wide, and 3 mm thick, attached to a base at 150 C with ambient air at 30 C and h = 25 W/m2K. Please provide: (1) Calculation of fin parameter m, corrected length Lc, and mL for insulated tip approximation, (2) Temperature distribution equation T(x) with numerical values at x = 25, 50, 75, 100 mm, (3) Heat transfer rate from a single fin using exact and approximate formulas with percentage error, (4) Fin efficiency and fin effectiveness with interpretation, (5) Total heat dissipation if 20 such fins are mounted on a 200 mm x 150 mm base, (6) Discussion of how fin geometry would change for a KTM 390 compared to a Bajaj Pulsar based on power density. Format the output as a numbered solution with a final results table and a short design insight paragraph. Consider these constraints: SI units throughout, follow the notation used in J.P. Holman and the R.K. Rajput Heat Transfer textbook common in Indian universities.

prompts.title: ANSYS Workbench Static Structural Tutorial
prompts.prompt: Act as a CAE engineer with 12 years of experience using ANSYS Workbench on structural simulations for Tata Motors vehicle components, now mentoring mechanical students. I need you to write a complete ANSYS Workbench 2023 R1 step-by-step tutorial for a beginner project. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student at SRM University doing my first FEA assignment. I need to perform a static structural analysis on a cantilever bracket made of IS 2062 E250 steel, 300 mm long, 100 mm wide, 10 mm thick, fixed at one end, with a downward 5 kN load at the free end. Please provide: (1) Geometry import or DesignModeler steps to create the bracket, (2) Engineering Data setup with E = 200 GPa, Poisson 0.3, yield 250 MPa, density 7850 kg/m3, (3) Meshing strategy with element size recommendation, mesh metrics to check, and refinement near fixed support, (4) Boundary conditions and load application with exact menu paths, (5) Solution setup to extract total deformation, equivalent von Mises stress, and safety factor, (6) Result interpretation with expected deformation and stress values for hand-calculation validation using PL3/3EI and Mc/I. Format the output as a numbered tutorial with exact ANSYS menu paths in bold and a validation table. Consider these constraints: assume student ANSYS license with node limit, and include tips to stay under 32000 nodes.

prompts.title: Manufacturing Process Selection Matrix
prompts.prompt: Act as a manufacturing process selection expert with 18 years of experience at L&T Heavy Engineering and now teaching at IIT Kharagpur. I need you to help me select the best manufacturing process for a given component using a weighted decision matrix. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student doing a mini-project to manufacture 5000 units per year of a connecting rod for a Mahindra 3-cylinder diesel engine. The part is 180 mm long, I-section, made from EN8 or 42CrMo4 steel, requiring UTS above 700 MPa and fatigue strength critical. Please provide: (1) A shortlist of 5 candidate processes: drop forging, precision forging, powder metallurgy, sand casting plus machining, and investment casting, (2) A weighted matrix with criteria like mechanical properties, dimensional accuracy, surface finish, tooling cost in INR, per-part cost in INR, lead time, and suitability for 5000 pcs/year, with weights summing to 100, (3) Scoring each process 1-10 with justification, (4) Final ranking and recommended process, (5) Rough tooling and per-piece cost estimate in INR assuming Pune or Chennai tier-1 supplier rates, (6) Comment on secondary operations like shot peening or heat treatment required. Format the output as a decision matrix table followed by a recommendation memo. Consider these constraints: Indian supplier ecosystem, reference ASM Handbook and PSG Design Data Book, and note any BIS standards applicable.

prompts.title: Material Selection for Automotive Part
prompts.prompt: Act as a materials science professor with 15 years of experience at IISc Bangalore who follows Ashby's material selection methodology. I need you to walk me through a material selection problem using performance indices. Here is the context: I am a 4th semester mechanical engineering student working on a course project to select material for a bicycle frame similar to Hero or Atlas urban commuter bikes sold in India, priced under INR 12000. The frame must be stiff in bending, light, low cost, corrosion resistant in Indian monsoon, and weldable in small workshops. Please provide: (1) Translation of design requirements into function, objective, constraint, and free variable, (2) Derivation of the performance index for minimum mass at given stiffness for a tube in bending (E^(1/2)/rho), (3) Shortlist of 5 candidate materials: plain carbon steel IS 1079, HSLA steel, 6061-T6 aluminium, AISI 304 stainless, and bamboo composite, (4) A comparison table with density, Young's modulus, yield strength, cost per kg in INR, performance index, and weldability rating, (5) Ashby chart style log-log interpretation in text, (6) Final recommendation for the Indian market with justification including local availability and repairability. Format the output as a structured report with tables and a final decision paragraph. Consider these constraints: real Indian supplier prices as of 2026, BIS standards, and affordability for Tier 2 and 3 city buyers.

prompts.title: AutoCAD 2D Drawing Setup Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as an AutoCAD instructor with 10 years of experience training mechanical engineering students at Anna University affiliated colleges. I need a complete setup and drafting tutorial for a first-year engineering graphics student. Here is the context: I just installed AutoCAD 2024 Student Edition and need to create an A3 sheet drawing of a stepped shaft with diameters 25, 40, 50, 40, 25 mm and lengths 30, 50, 40, 50, 30 mm with a 6 mm keyway on the 50 mm section, following Indian third angle projection and SP 46 standards. Please provide: (1) Workspace setup: units to millimetres, limits for A3 sheet 420x297, grid and snap, layers for visible, hidden, centre, dimension, hatch, and text with ISO linetypes and colours, (2) Text style and dimension style setup per SP 46 with arrow size 3, text height 3.5, ISO-25 dim style, (3) Step-by-step commands to draw front view, top view, and a sectional side view using LINE, OFFSET, TRIM, MIRROR, and HATCH, (4) Dimensioning procedure including diameter, linear, radial, and tolerance, (5) Title block creation with student name, roll number, scale, and date fields, (6) PDF export settings for A3 landscape at 1:1 scale. Format the output as a numbered tutorial with exact command prompts in monospace. Consider these constraints: follow SP 46 and BIS drafting standards taught in the AICTE mechanical engineering curriculum.

prompts.title: Otto Cycle Numerical With PV Diagram
prompts.prompt: Act as an IC engines and thermodynamics lecturer with 12 years of experience at PSG Tech Coimbatore who writes numerical problems for GATE coaching. I need you to solve an Otto cycle numerical from first principles. Here is the context: I am studying for my 4th semester thermodynamics exam. A four-stroke petrol engine similar to a Bajaj Pulsar 150 operates on an ideal Otto cycle with a compression ratio of 9.5. Air at the start of compression is at 100 kPa and 27 C. Heat addition at constant volume is 1800 kJ/kg. Treat air as ideal gas with Cv = 0.718, Cp = 1.005 kJ/kgK, gamma = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/kgK. Please provide: (1) P, V, T at all 4 state points in a clear table, (2) Work done during each process and net work per kg, (3) Thermal efficiency by both energy balance and by the formula 1 - 1/r^(gamma-1) as a cross check, (4) Mean effective pressure, (5) Comparison with the actual efficiency typically observed in the Pulsar 150 (around 25-28 percent) with reasons for the gap, (6) A text-based PV diagram description with process labels. Format the output as a full solved numerical with state table, equations, and a concluding insight paragraph. Consider these constraints: SI units, textbook notation from P.K. Nag, and highlight the GATE relevance of each step.

prompts.title: Fluid Mechanics Pipe Flow Problem
prompts.prompt: Act as a fluid mechanics professor with 15 years of experience at IIT Roorkee who has guided undergraduate projects on water supply systems in Indian cities. I need you to solve a complete pipe flow head loss problem with all intermediate steps. Here is the context: I am a 3rd year mechanical engineering student analysing a water distribution pipe from an overhead tank in a Chennai apartment building. Water at 30 C flows through a 50 mm diameter, 80 m long galvanised iron pipe (roughness 0.15 mm) at 2 L/s. The pipe has 4 standard 90 degree elbows (K = 0.9 each), one gate valve fully open (K = 0.17), and a sudden contraction from 75 mm to 50 mm at entry. Please provide: (1) Reynolds number and flow regime determination, (2) Friction factor using Colebrook equation with 3 iterations shown, and cross-check on Moody chart reading, (3) Major head loss using Darcy-Weisbach, (4) Minor head losses summed with a K-value table, (5) Total head loss and pump power required if flow is supplied by a 60 percent efficient pump, with cost per month of running at INR 7.5 per kWh assuming 8 hours daily, (6) Recommendation to reduce losses. Format the output as a structured solution with a final results box. Consider these constraints: use SI units, reference R.K. Bansal fluid mechanics textbook, and real Chennai electricity tariffs.

prompts.title: GATE ME Strategy Study Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a GATE ME topper AIR under 100 with 5 years of experience mentoring mechanical engineering students for GATE through Made Easy and ACE Academy. I need you to build me a personalised 6 month GATE ME preparation plan. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student at a Tier 2 college in Nagpur. My current strong areas are Thermodynamics and Fluid Mechanics (self-rated 7/10), average in Machine Design and Heat Transfer (5/10), weak in Theory of Machines, Manufacturing, and Industrial Engineering (3/10). I have 4 hours daily on weekdays and 8 hours on weekends. My target is AIR under 500 to get into an IIT M.Tech program. Please provide: (1) Subject-wise weightage based on last 5 years of GATE ME papers, (2) 26 week calendar with weekly topics mapped to chapters from Made Easy handbooks and NPTEL lectures, (3) Daily schedule template showing theory, practice problems, and revision time, (4) 4 full-length mock test milestones with target scores, (5) Revision and formula sheet strategy for the last 30 days, (6) Handling of the General Aptitude and Engineering Mathematics sections. Format the output as a markdown table week-by-week plus daily template. Consider these constraints: realistic for a hostel student with lab and project work, and recommend free Indian resources like NPTEL and GATE Overflow alongside paid ones.

prompts.title: Gear Train Velocity Ratio Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a Theory of Machines professor with 12 years of experience at NIT Warangal who has taught Rattan and S.S. Ratan's textbooks. I need you to solve an epicyclic gear train problem using the tabular (Willis) method. Here is the context: I am a 4th semester mechanical engineering student studying gear trains for my ToM exam. An epicyclic gear train has a sun gear S with 20 teeth, a planet gear P with 25 teeth, and an internal ring gear R. The arm A rotates at 200 rpm clockwise, and the sun rotates at 800 rpm counter-clockwise. Module is 2 mm. Please provide: (1) Calculation of the number of teeth on R using the geometric constraint, (2) Full tabular solution: step 1 fix arm and rotate sun, step 2 add arm rotation, showing rotations of S, P, R, and A, (3) Speed of the ring gear with direction, (4) Torque analysis using input torque of 50 Nm and assuming 95 percent efficiency, (5) Application example from a Maruti Suzuki automatic transmission where a similar train is used, (6) Common GATE question variants on this setup. Format the output as a clean tabulated solution with sign conventions explained. Consider these constraints: follow the notation of S.S. Rattan's Theory of Machines, which is standard in Indian curricula, and highlight where sign errors typically cost marks.

prompts.title: Refrigeration Cycle COP Calculation
prompts.prompt: Act as a refrigeration and air conditioning professor with 14 years of experience at Delhi Technological University who consults for Voltas and Blue Star. I need you to solve a vapour compression refrigeration cycle numerical with full p-h chart interpretation. Here is the context: I am a 6th semester mechanical engineering student working on my RAC lab report. A 1.5 TR split AC similar to a Voltas 1.5 ton 3-star model uses R32 refrigerant. Evaporator temperature is 7 C, condenser temperature is 45 C, subcooling of 5 C, and superheating of 5 C. Isentropic compressor efficiency is 80 percent. Please provide: (1) State points 1 to 4 with h, s, P, T values from R32 p-h chart or standard tables, (2) Refrigerating effect, compressor work actual and isentropic, condenser heat rejection, (3) Theoretical and actual COP, (4) Mass flow rate of refrigerant for 1.5 TR (5.27 kW) cooling load, (5) Power consumption and comparison with BEE 3-star EER benchmark for Indian climate, (6) Monthly running cost at INR 7 per unit assuming 8 hours daily use in a Delhi summer. Format the output as a solved numerical with state table, p-h diagram description, and cost summary. Consider these constraints: use BEE star rating norms and Indian tariff, follow C.P. Arora refrigeration textbook notation.

prompts.title: Welded Joint Strength Calculation
prompts.prompt: Act as a machine design professor with 15 years of teaching experience at Jadavpur University specialising in permanent joints. I need you to solve a welded joint strength problem using the PSG Data Book approach. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student designing a bracket welded to a wall to carry a load similar to equipment mounts seen in BHEL workshops. A rectangular plate 200 mm x 100 mm is welded on three sides to a vertical column with a 8 mm fillet weld. A load of 15 kN is applied at 300 mm from the weld plane, causing eccentric loading. The electrode is E70XX with permissible shear stress 95 MPa. Please provide: (1) Treating weld as a line, calculate polar moment of inertia Ip of the weld group about its centroid, (2) Primary shear stress due to direct load, (3) Secondary shear stress due to bending moment at the farthest point of the weld, (4) Resultant stress using vector addition with the angle between components, (5) Factor of safety against the permissible stress, (6) If unsafe, suggest a minimum weld size in 1 mm increments to achieve FoS of 1.5. Format the output as a step-by-step solution with a clear weld geometry sketch description. Consider these constraints: follow IS 816 welding code and PSG Design Data Book formulas used in Indian colleges.

prompts.title: Free Body Diagram Explainer
prompts.prompt: Act as an engineering mechanics tutor with 10 years of experience teaching 1st year mechanical engineering students at IIT Delhi. I need you to take a statics problem and produce a complete free body diagram and equilibrium solution suitable for a beginner. Here is the context: I am a 1st semester B.Tech student struggling with FBDs in my Engineering Mechanics course. The problem is a 5 m long uniform ladder of weight 200 N leaning against a smooth vertical wall at 60 degrees to the horizontal floor. A person of weight 700 N stands 3.5 m up the ladder measured along its length. The floor has coefficient of static friction 0.3. Please provide: (1) A text description of the FBD with every force labelled: ladder weight at midpoint, person's weight, normal from floor, normal from wall, friction at floor, with correct directions, (2) Three equilibrium equations sum Fx, sum Fy, sum M about the base, (3) Solve for all reactions step by step, (4) Check whether the ladder slips by comparing required friction to maximum available mu_s N, (5) Minimum coefficient of friction required for safety, (6) Beginner-friendly tips on choosing the moment point and sign conventions. Format the output as a tutorial with labelled FBD description, equations, and a highlighted final answer. Consider these constraints: use notation from S.S. Bhavikatti and H.J. Shah textbooks used in Indian 1st year B.Tech.

prompts.title: Stress-Strain Curve Interpretation
prompts.prompt: Act as a strength of materials professor with 12 years of experience at VJTI Mumbai specialising in material testing. I need you to interpret a stress-strain curve from a tensile test and extract all material properties a student must report. Here is the context: I am a 2nd year mechanical engineering student and just did a tensile test on a mild steel IS 2062 E250 specimen of 12 mm gauge diameter and 60 mm gauge length in the SOM lab. The recorded data shows: yield load 28.5 kN, upper yield 29 kN, lower yield 28 kN, ultimate load 42 kN at 18 percent engineering strain, fracture load 32 kN at 32 percent engineering strain, final gauge length 79 mm, final diameter at neck 8.5 mm. Please provide: (1) Engineering yield strength, UTS, fracture stress, (2) Percent elongation and percent reduction in area, (3) Modulus of elasticity estimation assuming elastic strain 0.12 percent at yield, (4) Approximate toughness and resilience with units, (5) True stress and true strain at ultimate and fracture, (6) A labelled description of the engineering stress-strain curve with all key points, and comparison with typical IS 2062 E250 specification values. Format the output as a lab report style results section with a table of properties. Consider these constraints: follow IS 1608 tensile testing standard and use notation from S. Ramamrutham's Strength of Materials.

prompts.title: FEA Meshing Best Practices Checklist
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior FEA analyst with 15 years of experience at Mahindra Research Valley working on vehicle BIW and chassis simulations. I need you to create a meshing best practices checklist for a student learning ANSYS or Hypermesh. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student at MIT Manipal preparing for an internship interview at a Tier 1 automotive supplier in Pune. I want to be confident about mesh quality, element selection, and mesh convergence. Please provide: (1) Element type selection table: when to use tet10, hex8, hex20, shell 181, beam 188, with real automotive examples, (2) Target mesh metrics with pass-fail thresholds: Jacobian, aspect ratio, skewness, warpage, minimum and maximum element size, (3) Mesh refinement zones: at fillets, holes, welds, load and boundary regions with recommended element counts, (4) Mesh convergence study procedure with stress monitoring at critical nodes and the 3 percent change rule, (5) Common meshing mistakes students make and how to avoid them, (6) A QA checklist of 15 items to run before submitting a solve. Format the output as a structured reference card with tables and bullet lists. Consider these constraints: focus on automotive static structural and crash simulation workflows relevant to Mahindra, Tata Motors, and Bosch India.

prompts.title: CNC G-Code Program Explainer
prompts.prompt: Act as a CNC programming instructor with 12 years of experience at a Mumbai tool room training CNC operators and mechanical students. I need you to write and fully explain a G-code program for a turning operation suitable for a CNC lab viva. Here is the context: I am a 5th semester mechanical engineering student with a CNC lab exam next week. The workpiece is a mild steel bar of 40 mm diameter and 100 mm length mounted on a Fanuc-controlled CNC lathe. I need to produce a stepped shaft: turn 30 mm diameter for 40 mm length, then 20 mm diameter for 30 mm length, with a 2 mm x 45 degree chamfer at the free end, and finish with 1.6 micrometre Ra. Please provide: (1) Complete G-code program in Fanuc format with line numbers, including tool change, spindle speed, feed, coolant, and safe start, (2) Line by line comments explaining each block, (3) Tool list with ISO designation, material, and recommended cutting parameters, (4) Cutting speed and feed calculations from Vc = pi D N / 1000, (5) Expected cycle time breakdown, (6) Common errors like wrong G90/G91 mode or missing M30 and how to catch them. Format the output as a code block followed by a commentary table. Consider these constraints: Fanuc 0i-TC controller commonly used in Indian engineering college labs, safety checks as per ITI training.

prompts.title: Final Year Project Topic Ideator
prompts.prompt: Act as a mechanical engineering project guide with 18 years of experience supervising B.Tech final year projects at NIT Surathkal, many of which led to publications and startups. I need you to suggest final year project topics tailored to my profile. Here is the context: I am a team of 4 final year mechanical engineering students at a Tier 2 college in Coimbatore. Our interests are thermal and renewable energy, budget is INR 15000 from college plus 10000 from us, we have access to a basic workshop, welding, 3D printer, and Arduino kits but no CNC or advanced CFD license. We want a project that is feasible in 6 months, publishable in a SCOPUS indexed conference, and relevant to Indian problems like energy access or agriculture. Please provide: (1) 8 project ideas across solar thermal, biogas, evaporative cooling, waste heat recovery, and micro hydro, (2) For each: problem statement, novelty angle, expected deliverables, required components with INR cost estimate, skills needed, and risk factors, (3) Rank them by feasibility and impact, (4) Top 3 recommendations with detailed week-by-week timeline, (5) Publication venues like ICMIE, SSRN, or UGC CARE list journals, (6) How to frame the work for a Smart India Hackathon or IIC grant. Format the output as a comparison table plus three detailed proposals. Consider these constraints: Indian agricultural context, realistic Tier 2 college resources, and AICTE project guidelines.

prompts.title: Composite Materials Laminate Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a composites professor with 14 years of experience at IIT Kanpur working on carbon fibre laminates for ISRO launch vehicles. I need you to solve a classical laminate theory problem at the introductory level. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student taking a composites elective. The laminate is a symmetric cross-ply [0/90/90/0] of T300/5208 carbon-epoxy with each ply 0.125 mm thick. Ply properties: E1 = 181 GPa, E2 = 10.3 GPa, G12 = 7.17 GPa, nu12 = 0.28. Applied in-plane loads: Nx = 1000 N/mm, Ny = 0, Nxy = 0. Please provide: (1) Reduced stiffness matrix [Q] for a single ply, (2) Transformed stiffness [Qbar] for 0 degree and 90 degree plies, (3) A, B, D matrices of the laminate, noting B = 0 due to symmetry, (4) Midplane strains and curvatures from ABD inverse, (5) Stress in each ply in laminate axes and in material axes, (6) First ply failure check using maximum stress criterion with Xt = 1500 MPa, Yt = 40 MPa, S = 68 MPa. Format the output as a structured solution with matrices displayed clearly and a final failure status statement. Consider these constraints: follow Autar Kaw composites textbook notation used in Indian graduate courses, and relate to real ISRO GSLV fairing applications.

prompts.title: Vibration SDOF System Solver
prompts.prompt: Act as a mechanical vibrations professor with 13 years of experience at IIT Guwahati using S.S. Rao's textbook. I need you to solve a damped single-degree-of-freedom forced vibration numerical with full derivation. Here is the context: I am a 6th semester mechanical engineering student studying for my vibrations exam. A machine similar to a Kirloskar reciprocating compressor of mass 80 kg is mounted on 4 springs each of stiffness 25 kN/m with a total damping coefficient of 800 Ns/m. A rotating unbalance produces a harmonic force F(t) = 200 sin(50t) N. Please provide: (1) Equivalent stiffness and natural frequency in rad/s and Hz, (2) Damping ratio and classification (under/critical/over damped), (3) Damped natural frequency, (4) Steady state amplitude using magnification factor formula, (5) Phase angle between force and response, (6) Transmissibility ratio and force transmitted to the foundation, with comment on whether isolation is achieved, (7) Recommendation for spring stiffness change to get transmissibility below 0.2. Format the output as a solved problem with formulas, substitution, and a final results table. Consider these constraints: SI units, follow S.S. Rao notation used across Indian B.Tech curricula, and highlight how this problem appears in GATE ME.

prompts.title: Metal Cutting Parameter Calculation
prompts.prompt: Act as a manufacturing science professor with 15 years of experience at IIT Kharagpur who researches metal cutting mechanics. I need you to solve a Merchant's circle and cutting force numerical with full explanation. Here is the context: I am a 5th semester mechanical engineering student doing my Manufacturing Science II assignment. Orthogonal turning of an AISI 1045 steel bar is done on a lathe with: uncut chip thickness 0.25 mm, width of cut 2.5 mm, rake angle 10 degrees, cutting speed 120 m/min, chip thickness after cut 0.6 mm, cutting force 1200 N, thrust force 450 N. Please provide: (1) Chip thickness ratio, shear plane angle using Merchant's relation, (2) Shear strain and shear strain rate, (3) Friction angle and coefficient of friction on the rake face, (4) Resultant force, shear force, normal force on shear plane using Merchant's circle geometry, (5) Specific cutting energy and power consumed, (6) Recommended cutting speed range and insert grade from ISO P25 carbide for Tata Steel AISI 1045, (7) Tool life estimate using Taylor equation with C = 200, n = 0.25. Format the output as a numbered solution with a Merchant's circle diagram described in text. Consider these constraints: follow Kalpakjian and Ghosh-Mallik textbooks used in Indian mechanical engineering programs.

prompts.title: MATLAB Script For Beam Deflection
prompts.prompt: Act as a computational mechanics tutor with 10 years of experience teaching MATLAB to mechanical engineering students at IIIT Hyderabad. I need you to write a fully commented MATLAB script that solves and plots beam deflection and bending moment diagrams for a simply supported beam. Here is the context: I am a 4th semester mechanical engineering student learning MATLAB for my SOM course. The beam is 6 m long, simply supported, carries a UDL of 15 kN/m over its entire length and a central point load of 40 kN. The beam is a standard ISMB 250 section with I = 5131.6 cm4 and E = 200 GPa. Please provide: (1) A single MATLAB script that takes inputs as variables at the top, (2) Reaction calculations, (3) Shear force and bending moment arrays along x from 0 to L with 1001 points, (4) Deflection using double integration solved numerically via cumtrapz, (5) Three subplots: SFD, BMD, deflection curve with labels, grid, and title in SI units, (6) Display maximum shear, maximum moment, maximum deflection to the command window with fprintf, (7) Inline comments explaining each block. Format the output as a single runnable .m file code block. Consider these constraints: must run on MATLAB R2022a student version, no toolboxes required, and use IS 808 section properties table for ISMB.

prompts.title: Casting Defect Root Cause Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a foundry engineer with 20 years of experience at Ashok Leyland's Ennore foundry and now teaching manufacturing at SASTRA University. I need you to walk a student through casting defect identification and root cause analysis. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student doing a manufacturing project at a local Coimbatore foundry making grey cast iron pump housings. Defects observed on recent castings include: porosity near the thick section, cold shut on the thin fin, shrinkage cavity at the top riser junction, and surface scabs on the parting line. Please provide: (1) A defect table with description, likely location in the casting, visible appearance, and typical inspection method, (2) Root cause analysis for each defect using a fishbone diagram structured as Man-Machine-Method-Material-Measurement-Environment, (3) Specific corrective actions for mould design, gating, venting, pouring temperature, and sand preparation, (4) Recommended changes to the methoding: riser size using Caine's method, gating ratio, chill placement, (5) Inspection checklist for the next 20 castings, (6) Estimated cost impact of rework and scrap in INR per casting. Format the output as a structured troubleshooting report with tables. Consider these constraints: typical Indian foundry using green sand and cupola furnace, follow IS 210 for grey iron grades, and reference P.N. Rao manufacturing textbook.

prompts.title: Heat Exchanger LMTD Design
prompts.prompt: Act as a heat transfer design engineer with 15 years of experience at BHEL Trichy designing shell and tube heat exchangers for thermal power plants. I need you to perform a complete LMTD based thermal design of a heat exchanger. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student doing a heat exchanger design mini-project. I need to design a 1-2 shell and tube heat exchanger to cool 5 kg/s of hot water from 90 C to 50 C using cold water available at 25 C that can be heated to 45 C, relevant to a dairy plant in Anand, Gujarat. Tubes are 19 mm OD, 1.65 mm thick, 3 m long, brass. Overall heat transfer coefficient can be assumed 1500 W/m2K initially. Please provide: (1) Heat duty in kW and cold water mass flow rate by energy balance, (2) LMTD calculation and correction factor F for 1-2 configuration with derivation of P and R parameters, (3) Required heat transfer area, (4) Number of tubes, tube bundle diameter with 1.25 pitch ratio, shell ID recommendation, (5) Pressure drop estimate on tube and shell side using Kern's method, (6) TEMA designation and material selection comment, (7) Rough cost in INR based on BHEL-like supplier rates. Format the output as a design report with a summary data sheet. Consider these constraints: use TEMA and IS 4503 standards common in Indian design offices.

prompts.title: Resume Builder For ME Internship
prompts.prompt: Act as a campus placement advisor with 12 years of experience helping mechanical engineering students at NITs land internships at Tata Motors, Mahindra, Bosch India, and L&T. I need you to rewrite my mechanical engineering internship resume to be ATS friendly and recruiter ready. Here is the context: I am a pre-final year mechanical engineering student at NIT Rourkela with CGPA 8.4, relevant coursework in Thermodynamics, Machine Design, FEA, Manufacturing, and MATLAB. My projects are: a Stirling engine prototype, an ANSYS FEA study of a car suspension arm, a SolidWorks model of a planetary gearbox. My skills are SolidWorks, ANSYS Workbench, AutoCAD, MATLAB, basic Python. I have a Coursera certificate in CATIA and was the SAE BAJA team brakes subsystem lead. I will supply my raw bullet points in my next message. Please provide: (1) A one page resume structure tailored for a 2 month summer internship in R&D or design at an Indian automotive OEM, (2) Strong action verbs and quantified bullet points under each project and role, (3) Skills section split into CAD, CAE, Programming, and Standards, (4) 4 ATS keywords to include per the target job, (5) 5 common mistakes to avoid, (6) A matching short LinkedIn headline and about section. Format the output as a ready-to-paste resume in markdown plus a revision checklist. Consider these constraints: Indian campus placement norms and typical OEM internship JDs.

prompts.title: Bolted Joint Preload Calculation
prompts.prompt: Act as a machine design professor with 14 years of experience at IIT Hyderabad who has consulted for ISRO on bolted flange joints. I need you to solve a pre-loaded bolted joint problem step by step. Here is the context: I am a 6th semester mechanical engineering student. A pressure vessel cover similar to those on a Godrej & Boyce storage tank is fastened by 8 bolts of M16 property class 8.8 on a 300 mm PCD. Internal pressure is 2 MPa acting on a 250 mm ID. The gasket is compressed asbestos with spring stiffness km = 4 x kb where kb is bolt stiffness. Please provide: (1) External load per bolt from pressure and effective area, (2) Joint stiffness factor C = kb/(kb+km), (3) Required preload using recommended 0.75 x proof load with proof strength 600 MPa and stress area 157 mm2, (4) Total bolt load under operation and check against proof load, (5) Separation factor and factor of safety against joint separation and fatigue, (6) Tightening torque using T = 0.2 x Fi x d with lubrication assumption, (7) Whether 8 bolts are sufficient or the count should increase. Format the output as a solved design problem with formulas and a decision statement. Consider these constraints: follow Shigley and IS 1367 bolt standards used in Indian engineering, and note GATE relevance.

prompts.title: 3D Printing Process Parameter Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as an additive manufacturing lab engineer with 8 years of experience running FDM and SLA printers at a fab lab in Bangalore supporting IISc student projects. I need you to recommend print parameters and a workflow for a specific part. Here is the context: I am a mechanical engineering student 3D printing a prototype of a drone quadcopter arm. The part is 180 mm long, hollow box section 20 x 20 mm, 2 mm wall, needs to carry a 400 g motor at the tip with minimal vibration. Printer is a Creality Ender 3 V2 with 0.4 mm nozzle and 220 C max hotend. Material choice is between PLA, PETG, and ABS from WOL3D or Fabheads. Please provide: (1) Material comparison table with cost per kg in INR, UTS, heat resistance, printability, and recommendation, (2) Optimal orientation for strength along the length with reasoning based on anisotropy, (3) Layer height, nozzle temperature, bed temperature, print speed, infill pattern and percentage, perimeters, top and bottom layers, (4) Support and brim strategy, (5) Post processing steps including annealing, (6) Estimated print time, filament consumption in grams, and total cost in INR, (7) Three design changes to improve strength-to-weight. Format the output as a Cura or PrusaSlicer ready parameter table with a short justification paragraph. Consider these constraints: Indian hobbyist filament availability and Ender 3 V2 limitations.

prompts.title: CFD Simulation Setup Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a CFD specialist with 12 years of experience running ANSYS Fluent simulations for Mahindra Aerospace wing design. I need you to give a step-by-step Fluent setup tutorial for a simple external aerodynamics case. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student at MNIT Jaipur analysing flow over a NACA 0012 airfoil at 10 degrees angle of attack, chord 1 m, freestream velocity 30 m/s, sea-level air. My goal is to extract lift and drag coefficients and compare to XFOIL. I have ANSYS 2023 R1 student version with cell count limit. Please provide: (1) 2D C-grid domain sizing recommendation (20c upstream, 30c downstream, 20c vertical) and meshing strategy with inflation layer for y+ below 1, target cell count under 512000, (2) Fluent setup: solver type pressure based steady, turbulence model k-omega SST with justification, (3) Boundary conditions for velocity inlet, pressure outlet, symmetry, airfoil wall, (4) Reference values setup to get Cl and Cd correctly, (5) Solution controls, residual targets, and report definitions for Cl, Cd convergence, (6) Post processing to extract pressure contours, velocity streamlines, Cp distribution, (7) Comparison values expected from XFOIL or Abbott data. Format the output as a numbered tutorial with exact Fluent panel paths. Consider these constraints: ANSYS Student 512K cell limit and typical Indian college lab GPU-less workstation.

prompts.title: Viva Voce Question Bank
prompts.prompt: Act as an external examiner with 20 years of experience conducting mechanical engineering viva voce at Mumbai University and VTU affiliated colleges. I need you to prepare a comprehensive viva preparation question bank with crisp model answers for a specific lab. Here is the context: I am a 5th semester mechanical engineering student with a Heat Transfer lab viva next week. The experiments I performed are: composite wall conduction, thermal conductivity of insulating powder, natural convection from a vertical cylinder, forced convection from a horizontal tube, emissivity of a test plate, Stefan-Boltzmann constant determination, parallel and counter flow heat exchanger, and pin fin with natural convection. Please provide: (1) 10 conceptual questions per experiment with 2-3 sentence model answers covering theory, working formula, typical results, and common errors, (2) 15 general heat transfer viva questions on dimensionless numbers, boundary layers, and modes of heat transfer, (3) 10 application based questions tying lab results to Indian industry like boilers, condensers, and insulation of LPG cylinders, (4) A list of 20 formulas to memorise with units, (5) 5 trick questions examiners commonly ask to test depth, (6) A 1 day revision schedule. Format the output as a structured Q&A document grouped by experiment. Consider these constraints: align with VTU and Mumbai University heat transfer lab syllabus and follow R.K. Rajput textbook notation.

prompts.title: Bearing Selection And Life Calculation
prompts.prompt: Act as a rolling element bearing specialist with 18 years of experience at SKF India and now teaching at College of Engineering Pune. I need you to select a bearing and calculate its L10 life for a given shaft application. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student designing a gearbox output shaft for a Mahindra tractor PTO. The shaft is 45 mm diameter at the bearing seat, rotates at 540 rpm, carries a radial load of 8 kN and an axial load of 2 kN. Target life is 10000 hours with 90 percent reliability. Please provide: (1) Type selection rationale: deep groove ball vs angular contact vs taper roller based on load ratio Fa/Fr, (2) Shortlist 3 SKF or NBC catalog bearings suitable for 45 mm bore, (3) Equivalent dynamic load using P = X Fr + Y Fa with X, Y from the catalog for the chosen bearing, (4) Dynamic load rating C required from L10 = (C/P)^3 x 10^6 / (60 N) formula and selection check, (5) Static load check and safety factor, (6) Lubrication recommendation, grease vs oil, relubrication interval for agricultural dusty environment, (7) Mounting method and shaft tolerance as per IS 2472. Format the output as a bearing selection report with catalog citation. Consider these constraints: real Indian bearing availability from SKF, NBC, FAG distributors in Pune, and tractor duty cycle factor of 1.2.

prompts.title: Solar PV Water Pump Feasibility
prompts.prompt: Act as a renewable energy systems engineer with 12 years of experience designing solar pumping systems for MNRE KUSUM scheme across Rajasthan and Gujarat. I need you to perform a feasibility and sizing calculation for a solar PV water pumping system for a student project. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student partnering with a farmer in Kota district. Requirement is 60000 litres per day lifted through a total head of 35 m for a 2 hectare wheat field. Solar insolation is 5.8 kWh/m2/day on average. Please provide: (1) Hydraulic power required and daily energy demand with pump efficiency 55 percent and motor efficiency 85 percent, (2) PV array sizing in kWp with derating factors for dust, temperature, and mismatch of 0.75, (3) Number and layout of 540 Wp Waaree or Adani mono PERC modules, (4) Pump selection: submersible vs surface with recommendation of a specific Kirloskar or CRI model, (5) MPPT controller and wiring specs, (6) Capex estimate in INR with KUSUM subsidy of 60 percent factored in, (7) Payback period vs diesel pump using INR 95 per litre diesel and 3 L/hour consumption, (8) Environmental impact in tonnes CO2 saved per year. Format the output as a feasibility report with a techno-economic table. Consider these constraints: MNRE KUSUM Component B guidelines and real Indian equipment prices.

prompts.title: Engineering Drawing Dimensioning Checker
prompts.prompt: Act as an engineering drawing expert with 15 years of experience as a design checker at L&T Heavy Engineering Powai and a guest faculty at VJTI Mumbai. I need you to review my engineering drawing description and give me an error checklist following SP 46 standards. Here is the context: I am a 2nd semester mechanical engineering student submitting my first part drawing assignment. I will describe the drawing of a stepped block with through holes and a slot, and I want you to list every dimensioning and projection rule I might have violated. My drawing is an A3 sheet, third angle projection, showing front, top, and side views of a 100 x 60 x 40 mm block with two 10 mm through holes centred 20 mm from each end, and a 20 mm wide x 5 mm deep slot across the top. I will paste my dimension list in the next message. Please provide: (1) A rule-based checklist of 25 common dimensioning errors as per SP 46: chain vs base dimensioning, redundant dims, missing radii, wrong arrow type, text orientation, tolerance placement, (2) Third angle projection view correctness checks, (3) Hidden line and centreline rules, (4) Title block mandatory fields per SP 46, (5) How to mark tolerance and surface finish symbols, (6) Common reasons drawings get rejected at L&T design review. Format the output as a numbered checklist grouped by category. Consider these constraints: SP 46, IS 10711, IS 11669 drawing standards.

prompts.title: Six Sigma DMAIC Student Project
prompts.prompt: Act as a Lean Six Sigma Black Belt with 15 years of experience leading projects at Bajaj Auto Chakan plant and now mentoring mechanical students. I need you to walk me through a DMAIC project suitable for a college mini project. Here is the context: I am a 7th semester mechanical engineering student at COEP Pune doing an Industrial Engineering project at a local auto-component MSME in Bhosari, Pune that machines brake drum castings. The current first-pass yield is 82 percent, with surface finish Ra above specification being the top defect (40 percent of rejections). Please provide: (1) Define phase: problem statement, SIPOC diagram outline, CTQ tree, project charter with goals in percentage and INR, (2) Measure phase: data collection plan, measurement system analysis with Gage R&R steps, current sigma level calculation, (3) Analyse phase: cause and effect diagram, 5 why analysis, Pareto of defects, hypothesis testing suggestion, (4) Improve phase: DOE with 3 factors (speed, feed, insert grade) at 2 levels using L8 Taguchi, (5) Control phase: control chart selection, updated SOP, poka yoke suggestion, (6) Expected savings in INR per year assuming 50000 parts output and INR 450 rework cost. Format the output as a project report structured by DMAIC with tools and deliverables per phase. Consider these constraints: MSME resource limits, ISO 9001 context, and Taguchi method as commonly taught in Indian IE courses.

prompts.title: Topology Optimization Problem Setup
prompts.prompt: Act as a structural optimization researcher with 12 years of experience using Altair OptiStruct and ANSYS topology tools at IIT Madras's CAD lab collaborating with Hindustan Aeronautics Limited. I need you to teach me how to set up a topology optimization problem for a student's design project. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student designing a lightweight bracket for mounting a camera on a quadcopter. Design space is a 120 x 80 x 40 mm solid block of AlSi10Mg for SLM printing. It must carry a 2 kg camera mass subjected to 10 g vertical loads, be fixed at 4 M4 bolt holes on one face, and minimise compliance. Target is 40 percent mass fraction. Please provide: (1) Problem formulation with objective, design variables, constraints, design and non-design spaces, (2) FE model setup recommendation: tet4 element size, mesh density, load application via RBE2, boundary conditions, (3) OptiStruct card image for DTPL, DOPTPRM, DCONSTR, DRESP1 with plain language explanation, (4) Manufacturing constraints for SLM: minimum member size, overhang angle 45 degrees, symmetry plane, (5) Iteration and interpretation of results, smoothing workflow to export STL via OSSmooth, (6) Post optimisation re-analysis and strength verification, (7) Expected mass reduction and stiffness retention percentages. Format the output as a step-by-step workflow with card snippets. Consider these constraints: Altair Student Edition node limits and realistic quadcopter load case.

prompts.title: IC Engine Performance Test Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as an IC engines lab in-charge with 14 years of experience at PES University Bangalore. I need you to help me write the analysis section of my IC engine performance lab report. Here is the context: I am a 6th semester mechanical engineering student. We tested a single cylinder 4-stroke diesel engine in the lab: bore 80 mm, stroke 110 mm, rated 5 kW at 1500 rpm, compression ratio 17.5, Rope brake dynamometer with drum diameter 300 mm, rope diameter 20 mm. Five readings: load in N: 0, 50, 100, 150, 200. Corresponding fuel consumption: 50 cc in 180, 120, 85, 65, 55 seconds respectively. Diesel density 830 kg/m3, CV 42000 kJ/kg, air flow via orifice measured as average 0.008 kg/s. Please provide: (1) Tabulated results of brake power, indicated power using Morse test assumption, mechanical efficiency, brake specific fuel consumption, brake thermal efficiency, volumetric efficiency for each load, (2) Performance curves description for BP vs BSFC, BP vs BTE, BP vs mech eff, (3) Heat balance sheet at full load, (4) Interpretation of trends with reasons, (5) Comparison with typical Kirloskar AV1 engine datasheet values, (6) Sources of error and improvements to the experimental setup. Format the output as a complete results and discussion section with tables. Consider these constraints: follow VTU and Anna University lab manual format and use R. Yadav IC engines textbook notation.

prompts.title: Kinematic Linkage Synthesis Problem
prompts.prompt: Act as a Theory of Machines professor with 13 years of experience at IIT Madras specialising in mechanism synthesis. I need you to solve a four-bar linkage synthesis problem for function generation using Freudenstein's equation. Here is the context: I am a 5th semester mechanical engineering student working on a mechanism design assignment. I need to synthesise a 4-bar linkage that generates the function y = log x in the range x = 1 to 10, with 3 precision points using Chebyshev spacing, input crank 60 to 120 degrees, output follower 30 to 90 degrees. Fixed link is 100 mm. Please provide: (1) Calculation of 3 precision points using Chebyshev spacing formula, (2) Conversion of x, y ranges to crank and follower angles at each precision point, (3) Three Freudenstein equations in K1, K2, K3, (4) Solve the 3x3 linear system by Cramer's rule or elimination, (5) Derive link lengths a (crank), b (coupler), c (follower) from K values, (6) Grashof criterion check for crank-rocker, (7) Structural error at 5 intermediate points and maximum error as percentage, (8) A mechanism schematic description. Format the output as a solved problem with equations, substitutions, and a results table. Consider these constraints: notation from S.S. Rattan and Shigley's mechanism design, and highlight GATE ME relevance.

prompts.title: Phase Diagram Microstructure Explainer
prompts.prompt: Act as a physical metallurgy professor with 16 years of experience at IIT BHU teaching undergraduate materials science. I need you to explain the Fe-Fe3C phase diagram and compute phase fractions for a given steel. Here is the context: I am a 3rd semester mechanical engineering student preparing for my materials science exam. The alloy is AISI 1045 steel (0.45 percent C) relevant to Tata Steel products used in Indian manufacturing. Please provide: (1) A text-based sketch of the Fe-Fe3C diagram with key points A1, A3, Acm, eutectoid 0.76 C at 727 C and eutectic 4.3 C at 1148 C labelled, (2) Cooling path of 1045 steel from 1000 C to room temperature with structural changes at each stage, (3) Lever rule calculation of phase fractions at 800 C (austenite+ferrite) and just below 727 C (pearlite+ferrite), and total ferrite vs cementite at room temperature, (4) Microstructure description with typical micrograph appearance, (5) Effect of cooling rate: annealing, normalising, quenching on microstructure and hardness, (6) Indian heat treatment practice at Mukand Ltd or Tata Steel and the resulting mechanical properties, (7) 5 likely GATE style questions on this topic. Format the output as a structured lecture note with tables. Consider these constraints: V. Raghavan Physical Metallurgy textbook notation used in Indian curricula.

prompts.title: Mohr's Circle Stress Analysis Walkthrough
prompts.prompt: Act as a strength of materials professor with 12 years of experience teaching at BITS Pilani Hyderabad campus. I need you to solve a Mohr's circle problem with full graphical and analytical steps. Here is the context: I am a 3rd semester mechanical engineering student learning 2D stress transformation. An element on a shaft surface has sigma_x = 80 MPa (tensile), sigma_y = -40 MPa (compressive), shear tau_xy = 30 MPa. The shaft is made from IS 2062 E250 steel with yield 250 MPa. Please provide: (1) Principal stresses sigma_1, sigma_2 and orientation theta_p analytically using the 2D transformation formulas, (2) Maximum in-plane shear stress and its orientation, (3) Mohr's circle construction: centre, radius, plot points X (sigma_x, -tau_xy) and Y (sigma_y, tau_xy), and a text-based coordinate description, (4) Rotation of the element by 30 degrees and stresses on the new faces, (5) Factor of safety using maximum shear stress (Tresca) and distortion energy (von Mises) criteria, (6) Commentary on which criterion is more conservative and why, (7) A small sketch-in-text of the original and principal element orientations. Format the output as a step-by-step solution with formulas, values, and a summary box. Consider these constraints: notation as per Ramamrutham and Timoshenko SOM textbooks used in Indian B.Tech curricula and highlight frequent GATE ME variants.

prompts.title: Interview Prep For Core ME Companies
prompts.prompt: Act as an ex-Tata Motors recruiter and design manager with 18 years of experience interviewing fresh mechanical engineers at campus drives in NITs and tier 1 private colleges. I need you to create a comprehensive technical interview prep kit for a final year student targeting core mechanical roles. Here is the context: I am a final year mechanical engineering student at NIT Warangal with shortlists from Tata Motors GET, Mahindra GMC, L&T ECC, and Ashok Leyland. My background is ANSYS, SolidWorks, SAE BAJA suspension lead, and GATE score 750. The technical round is 45 minutes, usually covering SOM, thermo, ToM, machine design, manufacturing, and project grilling. Please provide: (1) 40 high-yield technical questions grouped by subject, each with a model 3-4 sentence answer, (2) 10 application-based questions linking concepts to automotive systems like suspension, brakes, engine, transmission, (3) 10 project deep-dive questions on SAE BAJA likely to be asked, with structured STAR style answer templates, (4) 5 HR questions with Indian context (relocation, night shift at Pantnagar plant, bond agreement, expected CTC negotiation), (5) Red flags that get candidates rejected in Indian OEM interviews, (6) A 3 day last-mile revision plan, (7) Questions YOU should ask the panel to stand out. Format the output as a structured prep document with sections and a final checklist. Consider these constraints: real Indian OEM hiring practices, typical GET CTC bands of INR 6-9 LPA, and AICTE accredited college placement norms.
